steps.title: Identify the event
steps.content: The event is selecting a teen who talks on a cell phone daily. Use the column total for that event, because the question does not restrict the teen by texting behavior.

steps.title: Use favorable over total
steps.content: The table shows $415$ teens talk on a cell phone daily out of $799$ teens total. Therefore, $$P(\text{talks daily})=\frac{415}{799}$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A, $\frac{1}{799}$, is the probability of one particular teen, not this event.
  • Choice B, $\frac{415}{799}$, uses the daily-talk column total over the survey total. Correct.
  • Choice C, $\frac{384}{415}$, mixes the non-daily total with the daily total.
  • Choice D, $\frac{384}{799}$, is the probability of not talking daily.
That matches choice B.
